channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Police arraign three suspects for incest, defiling minors in Anambra
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description: The Anambra State Police Command has arraigned three suspects before the Gender Magistrate Court sitting in Awka for separate charges bordering on alleged incest, defilement, and rape following the conclusion of investigations. Among the suspects arraigned was one Ikenna Mojekwu, who is facing charg

item.title: Naira abuse: Randy Peter, Mama Pee return to EFCC for probe — Lawyer
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Activists Peter Akah and Mama Pee are back at the EFCC for further investigation into allegations of Naira abuse. Their lawyer confirms their cooperation.
Read More: https://punchng.com/naira-abuse-randy-peter-mama-pee-return-to-efcc-for-probe-lawyer/

item.title: Christians now more comfortable with Muslim-Muslim ticket, says APC chieftain
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
An APC chieftain claims Christians are now more comfortable with the Muslim-Muslim ticket, citing President Tinubu’s administration as dispelling fears of
Read More: https://punchng.com/christians-now-more-comfortable-with-muslim-muslim-ticket-says-apc-chieftain/

item.title: R. Kelly seeks clemency, asks Trump to reduce 31-year prison sentence
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
R. Kelly has formally appealed to President Trump for executive clemency, hoping to reduce his 31-year prison sentence for racketeering and sex trafficking
Read More: https://punchng.com/r-kelly-seeks-clemency-asks-trump-to-reduce-31-year-prison-sentence/

item.title: Why Igbo youths avoid army recruitment — IPOB counsel
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
IPOB counsel explains why Igbo youths avoid Nigerian army recruitment, citing historical marginalisation and distrust of the military.
Read More: https://punchng.com/why-igbo-youths-avoid-army-recruitment-ipob-counsel/

item.title: Ireland bring back big names for clash against All Blacks at Eden Park
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Ireland have brought back their big names for the crunch Nations Championship clash against the All Blacks at Eden Park on Saturday.

item.title: Norris to take 10-place Belgian GP grid penalty
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Lando Norris will take a 10-place grid penalty at Sunday's Belgian Grand Prix.

item.title: Plateau NLC To Picket House Of Assembly Over Lawmakers’ Pension Bill
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Plateau State chapter of the Nigeria Labour Congress (NLC) has vowed to picket the state House of Assembly over a bill seeking pension and retirement benefits for members of the legislature. NLC, backed by the Trade Union Congress (TUC) and the Coalition of Civil Society Organisations in Plateau

item.title: Obi’s ADC Exit Left South-East Politically Disadvantaged, Says Okonkwo
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: A chieftain of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Kenneth Okonkwo, has said that Peter Obi’s exit from the party left the South-East region disadvantaged and thrown into confusion during the process that led to the emergence of Rotimi Amaechi as the party’s vice-presidential candidate. Oko

item.title: Kwara Varsity Research Teams Secure Grants
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Four research teams from Kwara State University (KWASU), Malete, have secured competitive research grants from two renowned funding organisations. At the international level, a research team led by Dr Bolatito Olanipekun as principal investigator was awarded a $43,913 research grant by TWAS-UNESCO.

item.title: Lawyer Inibehe Effiong reveals why EFCC invited Randy Peter, Mama Pee
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Inibehe Effiong, the legal representative to Mama Pee qnd Randy Peter, has revealed why the duo were invited by the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission, EFCC. DAILY POST reports that the two activists and prominent supporters of Peter obi, presidential candidate for the Nigeria Democratic Congr

item.title: Gov Aliyu’s Solid Machinery For Victory
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The political machinery of Governor Ahmed Aliyu and Senator Aliyu Wamakko has single handedly shaped the direction of Sokoto State politics since 2007, and it fully understands better than anyone what it takes to win elections convincingly. Rather than relying solely on high-profile rallies and medi

item.title: Rabies Outbreak Claims 2 Lives In FCT, Govt Begins Vaccination Drive
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory Administration (FCTA) has confirmed an outbreak of rabies in parts of the territory, with two persons reported dead following bites from infected dogs. The deceased victims, one from Gwagwalada Area Council and the other from Kwali Area Council, died after contracting t

item.title: Court Denies Bail To Man Arrested By DSS For Planning Multiple Attacks On Abuja Schools
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has denied bail to a man arrested by the Department of State Services (DSS), for allegedly planning multiple attacks on several schools in the Federal Capital Territory (FCT). The post Court Denies Bail To Man Arrested By DSS For Planning Multiple Attacks On Abuja Sch

item.title: That Good Media to launch TGM Academy, Nigeria’s first dedicated talent management training institution, with Industry leadership roundtable
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: That Good Media (TGM), one of Nigeria's leading public relations and strategic communications agency with expertise spanning talent management, influencer sourcing, personal branding, media relations and reputation management, has announced the launch of TGM Academy, the country's first institution

item.title: CIIN President, Ilori, Bows Out
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Ebere Nwoji Outgoing President of the Chartered Insurance Institute of Nigeria (CIIN), Mrs. Yetunde Olubunmi Ilori,  has said that her vigorously pursued EPIC agenda has widely spread insurance education and awareness

item.title: UnitedHealth’s stock rallies on improved outlook for the year
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: UnitedHealth’s leadership pointed to “product design changes, improved medical management and better aligned pricing.”

item.title: BREAKING: Court Threatens to Foreclose Sowore’s Defence Over Continued Absence of Lawyers
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Justice Muhammed Umar of the Federal High Court sitting in Abuja has threatened to foreclose the defence of Omoyele Sowore if he fails to continue his defence. The judge made the order due to the absence of Sowore’s lawyer in court to bring more witnesses to testify on his behalf. At the resumed sit

item.title: Breaking: Court denies bail to man accused of planning attacks on Abuja schools
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has denied bail to a man arrested by the Department of State Services (DSS) for allegedly planning multiple attacks on several schools in the Federal Capital Territory. The post Breaking: Court denies bail to man accused of planning attacks on Abuja schools appeared f

item.title: Terror attacks: US lawmakers move to block all aid to Nigeria
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “I rise in strong support of my amendment to increase the withholding threshold for assistance to Nigeria from 50 per cent to 100 per cent,” he said.

item.title: How coach-on-pitch Messi undid England's tactics on the fly
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England pride themselves on their physicality, so shouldn't it have suited them to face a 39-year-old who spends most of his time walking? Not this 39-year-old. BBC Sport football tactics correspondent Umir Irfan explores a Lionel Messi masterclass.

item.title: Minister, Ex-Speaker Felicitate Osoba At 87
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Minister of State for Health and Social Welfare, Dr Iziaq Adekunle Salako and former speaker of Ogun State, Rt. Hon. Daisies Elemide, have paid glowing tribute to former governor of the state and elder statesman, Aremo Olusegun Osoba, as he celebrated his 87th birthday in Abeokuta, the state capital

item.title: ‘Our blood not worthless’ – Idoma diaspora demands urgent action over Benue killings
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Idoma Association USA has appealed to President Bola Tinubu to take immediate and decisive action to end the persistent killings in Benue South Senatorial District, describing the attacks on communities in Idoma land as a sustained campaign of violence that has lasted for more than a decade. In

item.title: Group Tasks Tinubu, National Assembly On Abuja Original Inhabitants’ Rights Law
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Network of Journalists on Indigenous Issues (NEJII) has asked President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and the National Assembly to work for a law that promotes the economic and cultural rights of Abuja’s original inhabitants. At a media conference in Lagos, NEJII said the Abuja first nations, comprisi

item.title: Two Dead in FCT Rabies Outbreak
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Olawale Ajimotokan in Abuja Two persons have died of Rabies outbreak caused by a rabid dog bite in Abuja. The Director of Veterinary Services in the secretariat, Dr Karnak Dandam, confirmed

item.title: Uber Launches $14.8bn Bid for Delivery Hero to Expand Global Food Delivery Business
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Uber has launched a $14.8 billion takeover offer for Germany’s Delivery Hero, a deal that could create the world’s largest food delivery platform outside China and expand Uber’s delivery operations globally. The post Uber Launches $14.8bn Bid for Delivery Hero to Expand Global Food Delivery Business

item.title: Why you should care about World Cup third-place play-off
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: It might feel like the last thing anyone wants right now - but here is why, actually, you do want to watch the World Cup's third-place play-off.

item.title: 100,000 companies risk removal from CAC register
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The exercise, designated Batch 6, is being undertaken in line with Section 692 (3) and (4) of the Companies and Allied Matters Act (CAMA), 2020.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Resilient and Inclusive Agri-Food Systems Empowering Women and Youth in Nigeria (RISE) Project has opened applications for women and youth-led agribusinesses in Bauchi, Kaduna and Kano States to access financial and technical support to expand businesses, create jobs and promote climate-smart ag

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) has commenced another round of removing inactive and non-compliant companies from Nigeria’s register of incorporated businesses, targeting 100,000 companies in the latest exercise. The commission, in a public notice issued on Wednesday, July 15 said the a

item.title: Dangote Refinery shields Nigerians from global fuel price shock – S&P
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: According to S&P Global, the cost of transporting clean petroleum products from Northwest Europe to West Africa has risen from US$29.70 per metric tonne at the end of June to US$37.12 per metric tonne, as vessels reposition to serve alternative markets. The post Dangote Refinery shields Nigerian

item.title: Osun APC Candidate Assures British High Commission Of Commitment To Peaceful Election

item.title: Plateau Youths Demand Justice Over Killing Of 50 Residents In 1 Week
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Indigenous Youth Associations (IYA) in Plateau State have stated that over 50 persons have been killed within one week in renewed attacks on communities in Riyom and Bassa local government areas, with hundreds of residents displaced. They called on the federal government to ensure justice for the vi

item.title: Bogo Beverages Inaugurates N20bn Manufacturing Plant
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Raheem Akingbolu Bogo Beverages has inaugurated  a new 80,000-capacity manufacturing plant in Ikorodu, Lagos, following a N20 billion investment aimed at expanding its footprint in Nigeria’s fast-growing beverage industry. The inauguration  ceremony

item.description: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) has commenced the sixth batch of its company deregistration exercise, placing 100,000 companies at The post CAC begins sixth batch of company strike-off, targets 100,000 firms appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) has commenced another nationwide exercise to strike off 100,000 companies from its register for failing to comply with statutory filing requirements under the Companies and Allied Matters Act (CAMA), 2020. In a public notice, the Commission announced its intent

item.title: Umahi Demands Autopsy To Unravel Cause Of Aide’s Death
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, has formally asked the Commissioner of Police in Ebonyi State to ensure that a comprehensive forensic autopsy is conducted on the remains of one of his staff members, Miss Mary Habila. According to Umahi, the autopsy would scientifically determine the caus

item.title: Ex-Aso Villa chaplain dies, CAN mourns
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN) has mourned the death of former Aso Rock Villa Chaplain and Founder of All Christians Fellowship Mission, Rev. Dr. William Uzochukwu Okoye, describing him as 'a faithful shepherd' whose passing is a great loss to the Church in Nigeria. The post Ex-Aso Villa

item.title: AbdulRazaq Seeks Action Plan To Harness Kwara’s Demographic Dividend
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Kwara State governor, AbdulRahman AbdulRazaq, has called for a practical, time-bound action plan to harness the state’s demographic dividend. The governor stressed that Kwara must deliberately invest in its youthful population to achieve sustainable economic growth. AbdulRazaq, represented by

item.title: US imposes a 25 percent tariff on some Brazilian imports, citing unfair trade practices
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The United States will slap 25 percent tariffs on some Brazilian goods starting July 22 following a yearlong investigation under the Trade Act of 1974. The probe concluded that Brazil engaged in unfair trade practices. Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva condemned the move, saying it was p

item.title: Iran slams ‘barbaric’ US attack after hospital evacuated due to nearby strike
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Tehran on Thursday accused the United States of carrying out a "barbaric attack" after a cancer hospital in Iran's southwest was forced to evacuate due to nearby strikes. The post Iran slams ‘barbaric’ US attack after hospital evacuated due to nearby strike appeared first on Vanguard New

item.title: Nurses Give FG 15-Day Ultimatum, Threaten Nationwide Warning Strike

item.description: The National Association of Nigeria Nurses and Midwives (NANNM), Federal Health Institutions (FHI) Sector, has issued a 15-day ultimatum to the Federal Government, warning that it will embark on a three-day nationwide warning strike if its demands are not met by July 29. In a letter addressed to the

item.description: Along with the fatalities, 19 people suffered injuries, the authorities say.

In a public notice signed by its management on Wednesday, the CAC said the firms failed to file outstanding annual returns and other statutory documents. According to t

item.title: Ukrainians protest the dismissal of Kyiv's 'most successful defence minister'
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Russian missiles targeted the Ukrainian capital early on July 16, but it hasn't deterred protesters who are out in the streets opposing the dismissal of the popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov. During his tenure, Federov oversaw a series of successful Ukrainian operations, including strikes o

item.title: Court Jails Man For Advertising Cannabis On Social Media
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: A Federal High Court in Kano has sentenced a 28-year-old man, Ashiru Idris, to seven years imprisonment without the option of a fine for advertising cannabis sativa for sale on social media. The conviction was secured by the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA), which described the ruling as

item.title: FRSC Act: Senate raises traffic fines by 300%
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigerian motorists and commuters will soon pay more for traffic offences as the Senate has passed amendments to the Federal Road Safety Commission, FRSC, Act introducing stiffer penalties. The post FRSC Act: Senate raises traffic fines by 300% appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI (OPHK) have apprehended suspected terrorist informants and logistics suppliers, received the surrender of an ISWAP member and destroyed improvised explosive devices (IEDs) in the last 24 hours across Borno. item.title: Congress moves to block US assistance to Nigeria over FG’s failure to protect citizens
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The United States House of Representatives has approved an amendment that would increase the withholding of certain US assistance to Nigeria from 50 percent to 100 percent until the country takes effective steps to address violence and improve the protection of its citizens. The amendment, proposed

item.dc:creator: Channels TV

item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu is set to visit Morocco on July 16 in an effort to strengthen ties between Paris and Rabat, whether it is for trade or defence. But as the visit starts, an investigation has revealed that Morocco has used Israeli spyware to spy on several French ministers, inc

item.title: 2027: Gov. Yusuf Rewards APC Polling Unit Supporters With 1,540 Plots, N100m
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Kano State Governor, Alhaji Abba Kabir Yusuf, has distributed 1,540 plots of land and a cash grant of ₦100 million to leaders of the APC Polling Unit Supporters Association, popularly known as ’Yan Akwati, as part of efforts to strengthen the party’s grassroots structure ahead of the 2027 general el

item.title: FA To Retain Tuchel Despite England’s World Cup Defeat
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Football Association (FA) is set to retain Thomas Tuchel as England manager ahead of the 2028 UEFA European Championship despite the Three Lions’ semi-final exit at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Football transfer expert Fabrizio Romano disclosed the development in a post on X, stating that ther

item.title: Kano, Jigawa Customs generated N63.07bn in six months -Controller
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kano/Jigawa Customs Area Command generated N63.07bn in revenue from January to June 2026, exceeding 2025 figures. Learn more about Kano Customs revenue and
Read More: https://punchng.com/kano-jigawa-customs-generated-n63-07bn-in-six-months-controller/

item.title: Messi dethrones Yamal as 2026 World Cup’s top dribbler (full list)
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The eight-time Ballon d’Or winner also produced two assists as Argentina recovered from a goal down to eliminate England and reach another World Cup final.

item.title: French Power Exports Jump 36% to Record on Nuclear Output Boost
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: French power exports surged to a record in the first half of 2026 as a rebound in nuclear output and rising renewable generation left the country with a big surplus of low-cost electricity to sell across Europe.

item.title: Broker Alpaca Raises $435 Million, Explores Prime Broking Entry
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Brokerage and financial infrastructure startup Alpaca has raised $135 million in equity and $300 million in debt, as it looks to expand a business that helps investors trade across financial markets using artificial intelligence agents.

item.title: Peter Obi’s ‘unceremonious’ exit from ADC left Southeast disadvantaged, confused – Okonkwo
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Okonkwo said he was satisfied after the party explained the rationale behind the pick, adding that internal negotiations were ongoing to address any lingering reservations within the party. The post Peter Obi’s ‘unceremonious’ exit from ADC left Southeast disadvantaged, confused &#

item.title: Nigeria to track poverty, income, inequality in new prosperity scorecard, Oyedele says
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Nigeria will soon begin measuring the success of its economic reforms using a new scorecard tracking poverty, real incomes and read more Nigeria to track poverty, income, inequality in new prosperity scorecard, Oyedele says

item.title: Messi, Argentina React After Discovering Pickford’s Penalty Cheat Sheet
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Lionel Messi and his Argentina teammates were left intrigued after discovering England’s goalkeeper Jordan Pickford’s penalty cheat sheet following their World Cup semi-final victory over England, revealing the Three Lions had prepared extensively for the possibility of a shootout. After

item.title: A week of strikes shatters hope of a lasting US-Iran peace deal
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Hopes of a lasting peace deal between Iran and the US are fading fast following a week that has seen consistent exchange of fire between the two sides. The US resumed its blockade on the Strait of Hormuz firing at a ship that attempted to breach it. For its part, Iran retaliated by launching strikes

item.title: Content creator arrested over 20-year-old woman’s suicide
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Favour, before committing suicide, made a video detailing her encounter with Ogbonna.

item.title: APGA Hails Tinubu Over Approval Of Otuocha-Anam-Abaji Road Project
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The All Progressives Grand Alliance (APGA) has commended President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for approving the construction of the 180-kilometre Otuocha–Anam–Abaji Road, describing the decision as a demonstration of progressive politics. The party also welcomed the approval for the reconstruction of the Oba

item.title: Turbo-charged SK Hynix volatility shows no sign of abating as AI euphoria swings to fatigue
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: SK Hynix shares continued their rollercoaster ride Thursday with a 12% collapse following on from Wednesday’s near 9% jump.

item.title: What Nigerians Need For Democratic Governance
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Idegu Ojonugwa Shadrach Sir, life, as we see, is deeper beyond logical knowledge. Imaginative knowledge, which is the fountain of rapid transformation, is encouraged to encounter the presence of ethical intellectualism directed to making citizens of a staggering nation The post What Nigerians Nee
